violations.text: At the time of inspection, shrimp stored in metal bowl in the walk-in cooler was observed being dripped into by the refrigeration unit condensate line. The shrimp were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025

Scoops with handles used for dispensing dry ingredients were observed stored with the handles touching the food item. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Scoops were adjusted for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in poor repair... - The walk-in cooler door gasket was observed to be torn/ripped - The walk-in cooler refrigeration unit condensation drainpipe was observed to be leaking Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

The painted ceiling in the kitchen area was observed to have peeling paint and in need of repair. At the time of inspection, proper surfaces characteristics were reviewed with the PIC. The physical facilities must be maintained smooth, durable, easily cleanable, non-absorbent, and in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

At the time of inspection, pans were observed being stored in the food prep sink. Utensils/equipment are to be stored in a manner to prevent possible contamination. Pans were moved at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024
